items.title: Chicken and eggs could end up cheaper because of new high-protein sorghum variety
items.summary: Australian researchers develop a high-protein sorghum variety, hoping to replace expensive livestock feed with systems that could lead to cheaper meat and eggs.

items.summary: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi smashes a 15-ball half-century as he leads Rajasthan Royals to an easy victory over Chennai Super Kings.

items.title: Amid fuel price rises, business owners plead for kindness as abuse from customers increases
items.summary: From pile-on comments on social media to roadside slurs, local businesses are reminding their communities to "be kind to the little guy" amid the fuel crisis.

items.summary: Israel is planning to occupy Lebanon's south and repeat what it did in Gaza, according to Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz. Follow live.

items.title: From Missy Higgins to a succulent Chinese meal, these are the 2026 Sounds of Australia
items.summary: A variety of iconic Australian recordings are added to the Sounds of Australia collection, as part of an annual update by the National Film and Sound Archive.

items.title: Hobart City Council to introduce safety liaison officers following rise in antisocial behaviour
items.summary: A trio of council-employed public safety monitors will soon be patrolling "hot spots" in the Hobart CBD, to try to curb antisocial behaviour and theft.

items.title: Dezi Freeman death brings end to mammoth seven-month police operation

items.title: Italy's World Cup fate to be decided, Socceroos to learn final group opponent
items.summary: The last of the 48 teams for the FIFA World Cup will be decided tomorrow, with Italy hoping to end a 12-year exile, while Türkiye and Kosovo look to join the Socceroos' group.

items.title: How one man's stolen identity was used by the man accused of running an international cult from Nannup

items.title: 50 per cent of Australians avoid health care they need due to cost, according to Consumers Health Forum report
items.summary: One in two Australians missed out on health care they needed last year mainly because they could not afford it, according to a new report by the peak healthcare consumers group.

items.title: Early government modelling assumes fuel rationing when stockpile falls to 10 days' diesel supply

items.title: Fuel purchasing powers could be used for nearly anything facing supply disruption

items.title: Insiders profit as Iran war pressure points build on global economy

items.title: Bittersweet Iraqi film The President's Cake brings hope as war rages in the Middle East
items.summary: Filmmaker Hasan Hadi had to bring flowers to school to celebrate Saddam Hussein’s birthday. That memory loosely informs his beautiful debut feature, The President’s Cake.

items.title: If your home is underinsured it comes with big risks, experts say
